title: Walkthrough: Able Assess Falls Risk Tests

seo.title: How Able Assess Works: Four Falls Risk Tests in Five Minutes | Able Care
seo.description: See how Able Assess runs four validated falls risk tests in one five-minute screening any staff member can deliver. Grip strength, Timed Up & Go, gait speed, chair stand.

blocks.headline: Four falls risk tests. One five-minute screening.
blocks.subtitle: Run by any staff member. Validated against CDC STEADI and the 2022 World Guidelines for Falls Prevention. No clinical certification required.
blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Able Assess running on a tablet next to the GripAble sensor

stats.label: Assessments delivered
stats.sublabel: Across home care, senior living, and clinical sites

blocks.heading: Four key falls risk assessments in five minutes
blocks.body:
One sensor, one app, one workflow. The same five-minute screening covers all four measurements the 2022 World Guidelines call for:

  • Single Maximum Grip Strength
  • Timed Up & Go Test
  • Four-Meter Gait Speed
  • 30-Second Chair Stand Test

Two minutes of footage shows what an actual screening looks like, from app open to participant report.

blocks.heading: The sensor at the center of every test.
blocks.description: Designed for precision. Built for real-world care. The same sensor measures grip force in the hand and movement in the fanny pack, with no calibration between tests.

cards.title: The kit
cards.body: A GripAble sensor, a measuring tape, and the blue fanny pack that holds the sensor during the gait, balance, and chair-stand tests. Ships ready to use.

cards.title: The Able Assess app
cards.body: Walks any staff member through every test, step by step. Fifteen minutes of training, no clinical certification needed.

cards.title: The Able Assess portal
cards.body: See risk stratification across your population, and track changes for any individual over time. Export reports for care plans, family meetings, or audits.

blocks.imageAlt: Close-up of a hand gripping the GripAble sensor
blocks.headline: One sensor, four tests
blocks.description: The sensor measures grip force when held in the hand, and movement, balance, and tempo when worn in the fanny pack. No calibration between tests, no swapping equipment, no setup time between participants.

bulletPoints.text: Bluetooth pairs to any iOS or Android tablet
Drop-proof, ten-hour battery, three-year service life

steps.title: Open the app
steps.description: Pick the participant. The app walks you through the four tests in order. Every prompt is on screen. No script to memorize.

steps.title: Run the four tests
steps.description: For grip strength, the participant squeezes the sensor when the app says go. For gait speed, lower-limb strength, and balance, you measure the distance the app shows you, slip the sensor into the fanny pack, and follow the on-screen instructions to guide the participant.

steps.title: See results immediately
steps.description: The app stratifies each participant into low, intermediate, or high falls risk against age-banded norms. Print a participant-friendly paper report on the spot if needed.

steps.title: Track changes over time
steps.description: Every screening syncs to the Able Assess portal. Pull individual trends for care planning, or population reports for compliance, board, and operator reviews.

blocks.imageAlt: Able Assess software showing risk stratification across a population
blocks.headline: Results that travel
blocks.description: Risk band on screen at the moment of the test. Paper report on demand for the participant or family. Population view in the portal for the operator. Every screening flows automatically from sensor to portal, with no manual transcription.

bulletPoints.text: Instant low / intermediate / high risk band
Printable participant report in plain language
Population dashboard with branch, region, and date filters
FHIR R4, OASIS-E, and HL7v2 integration ready

metrics.name: Single Maximum Grip Strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Peripheral marker of muscle mass, bone density, and biological resilience. The Lancet (2015) showed grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

metrics.name: Timed Up & Go (TUG)
metrics.whatItMeasures: Dynamic balance and functional mobility. One of the most widely validated falls risk tests in the literature, captured in under a minute.

metrics.name: Four-Meter Gait Speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to mortality, hospitalization, and functional decline. Distance is set with the included measuring tape.

metrics.name: 30-Second Chair Stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb strength and endurance. A direct indicator of fall likelihood and a leading marker of functional decline.

blocks.heading: Why these four tests
blocks.body: These four measurements together stratify risk more accurately than any single one. Grip strength is a peripheral marker of muscle mass, bone density, and biological resilience. Timed Up & Go captures dynamic balance. Four-meter gait speed measures functional locomotion. The 30-second chair stand isolates lower-limb strength. Captured together, they produce the multifactorial picture the 2022 World Guidelines for Falls Prevention call for, in five minutes instead of forty-five.

blocks.heading: Backed by the research
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
citations.finding: Grip strength was a stronger predictor of all-cause and cardiovascular mortality than systolic blood pressure across 17 countries.

citations.title: Multifactorial falls risk screening outperforms single-test approaches
citations.finding: Multifactorial assessment combining strength, balance, and gait measures identified significantly more at-risk older adults than any single screening tool.

citations.title: Objective screening reduces falls incidence by 25%
citations.finding: Standardized, objective falls risk screening programs reduced falls incidence by approximately 25% in community-dwelling older adults.

citations.title: Hand grip strength as a proposed new vital sign of health
citations.journal: Journal of Health and Population Nutrition
citations.finding: Grip strength proposed as a new vital sign of health, with evidence supporting its role across the lifespan.

citations.title: Functionally impaired patients face up to 42% higher readmission risk
citations.finding: Functional impairment at discharge was independently associated with a 42% increased risk of 30-day hospital readmission.

blocks.heading: Able Assess vs paper-based assessment
blocks.tldr: Paper assessments take 20+ minutes, are scored subjectively, and rarely make it into the care plan. Able Assess runs in under five, scores automatically, and routes results to the team.

caseStudies.title: Home care: earlier intervention, fewer hospitalizations
caseStudies.summary: A PE-backed home care operator rolled out Able Assess across 12 branches. Non-clinical staff ran screenings in under five minutes, flagging at-risk clients six weeks earlier than their previous paper-based process.

caseStudies.title: Senior living: protecting occupancy through prevention
caseStudies.metricLabel: fewer falls per resident per year
caseStudies.summary: A CCRC with 200 IL and AL units adopted Able Assess as part of their quarterly wellness program. Resident engagement increased and fall-related move-outs dropped.

caseStudies.title: Clinical research: objective endpoints for decentralized trials
caseStudies.metricLabel: validated functional endpoints in one protocol
caseStudies.summary: A CRO-led GLP-1 trial used Able Assess to capture grip strength and functional health remotely, replacing site-based Jamar assessments with digital, standardized capture.

blocks.heading: What the people running it say

testimonials.quote: We increased our caseload by 20% by identifying patients at risk of falls earlier than we would otherwise.
The simplicity of this product is what makes it valuable. Anybody can use it, and we can trust that the data is accurate with every assessment.

blocks.heading: How much could you save?
blocks.promoBody: Model HHVBP upside, hospitalization avoidance, and the cost of one prevented fall in 60 seconds.

blocks.heading: The Buyer's Guide to Functional Assessment

faqs.question: What hardware does Able Assess require?
faqs.answer: One GripAble sensor, one tablet (iOS or Android), and the kit that ships in the box: a measuring tape and the blue fanny pack that holds the sensor during gait, balance, and chair-stand tests. No specialist equipment beyond the kit.

faqs.question: Can non-clinical staff really run this?
faqs.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built for non-clinical staff. Fifteen minutes of training, no clinical certification required. The app guides every prompt on screen, scores automatically, and produces the report. Staff do not need to interpret results in the moment.

faqs.question: How long does a full screening take?
faqs.answer: Under five minutes for an experienced staff member. The four tests are designed to flow into each other with no calibration or equipment swap between them.

faqs.question: How are results shared with families and care teams?
faqs.answer: Three ways. A printable plain-language report on the spot. A secure share link the participant can forward. A direct integration into the care plan via FHIR R4, OASIS-E, or HL7v2. Recipients do not need an account to view a shared report.

faqs.question: How is Able Assess different from a paper falls risk assessment?
faqs.answer: Paper assessments are subjective, slow, and difficult to standardize across staff. Able Assess captures four objective, peer-reviewed metrics in under five minutes, scores automatically against age-banded norms, and routes results to the care team. No subjective scoring. No inter-rater variability.

faqs.question: How is Able Assess different from a fall detection pendant?
faqs.answer: Pendants react after a fall has happened. Able Assess screens for risk before a fall happens. The two are complementary, not competing. Able Care sits at the prevention layer, upstream of detection and response.

faqs.question: Where is patient data stored?
faqs.answer: Able Care is FDA listed, CE marked, ISO 13485 certified, and HIPAA aligned. Data is encrypted at rest and in transit. US and UK data residency options are available.

faqs.question: How long until we are running our first screening?
faqs.answer: Most teams run their first screening on the same day the kit arrives. Setup is Bluetooth pairing plus the fifteen-minute training video. No environment setup, no calibration.
